title: Alnylam — AHP — briefing Q1-2026
html:
GIVLAARI / AHP Briefing: Q1 2026

Period covered: January - March 2026 Key data sources: Alnylam FY2025 earnings (Feb 12, 2026), Alnylam 2030 strategy launch (Jan 11, 2026), ATU Wave 5 planning, market landscape updates

Headline

Alnylam's FY2025 results confirm GIVLAARI + OXLUMO at $500M combined revenue (+18% YoY) with 15% patient growth for GIVLAARI. The Alnylam 2030 strategy launches with >25% CAGR targets anchored on TTR, with rare disease positioned for mid-single-digit growth. ZoomRx should anticipate ATU Wave 5 scope finalization and advocate for an expanded research agenda before budget decisions are locked.

1. Commercial Performance

FY2025 Full Year Results (Feb 12, 2026)

  • GIVLAARI + OXLUMO combined: $500M in FY2025 revenue, up 18% from FY2024
  • Q4 2025: $137M combined quarterly revenue, up 26% YoY -- strongest quarter
  • GIVLAARI patient growth: ~15% YoY, driven by new patient starts
  • 2026 rare disease guidance: Mid-single-digit growth -- notable deceleration from the 18% growth in 2025

Revenue Context

GIVLAARI is performing as expected for a mature rare disease brand: steady growth driven by patient identification, not competitive dynamics. The mid-single-digit 2026 guidance suggests Alnylam expects the pace of new patient starts to slow unless diagnostic acceleration programs yield results.

Key question for ZoomRx: Is the 15% patient growth rate sustainable? If new patient starts slow, GIVLAARI's growth will depend on:

  1. Expanding into Patient Types 4/5 (requires payer access shifts)
  2. Reducing the diagnostic delay (requires disease awareness investment)
  3. Improving Brazil/OUS access (requires structural interventions)

Alnylam 2030 Strategy (Jan 11, 2026)

  • Targets: >25% revenue CAGR through 2030, 30% non-GAAP operating margin
  • Growth engine: TTR franchise (AMVUTTRA) -- not rare disease
  • Rare disease = stable base, not growth driver
  • Implication: GIVLAARI research budgets may face scrutiny. ZoomRx needs to position research as high-efficiency, high-ROI tracking rather than large-scale exploratory studies

What Alnylam management said on the Q1 2026 call:

  1. GIVLAARI received no specific management commentary on the Q1 2026 call — the product was included in the combined rare disease franchise number (~$80M quarterly for GIVLAARI) without individual discussion.
  2. The absence of commentary is itself consistent with the "stable base" positioning established in prior quarters.
  3. Mid-single-digit 2026 growth guidance for the rare disease franchise was reiterated without any GIVLAARI-specific catalysts mentioned.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions on AHP or GIVLAARI — third consecutive quarter with zero analyst attention on this franchise.
  2. This sustained absence of analyst interest confirms GIVLAARI is fully mature in the investment narrative. Any research budget advocacy for the GIVLAARI engagement will need to come from the brand team, not from investor pressure.

2. Market Landscape Update

Disease Understanding: The Chronicity Paradigm Is Now Mainstream

The shift ZoomRx documented in ATU Wave 4 (2024) is becoming mainstream in the medical literature and clinical practice:

  • A 2026 review article on givosiran for AHP treatment confirms that "givosiran has redefined the therapeutic landscape of AHP" and notes considerable disease burden in patients regardless of annualized attack rates -- validating the chronic disease framing
  • An ASH Hematology 2024 education piece explicitly positions givosiran as "a targeted treatment for acute intermittent porphyria" that addresses both attacks and chronic disease manifestations
  • AGA Clinical Practice Update (2023, still current) recommends evaluation and management of AHP that accounts for chronic symptoms and long-term complications alongside acute attacks

Implication: The clinical community has caught up with what the ZoomRx data showed in Wave 4. GIVLAARI's value proposition as a chronic disease management tool (not just attack prevention) now has broader medical consensus. This should strengthen Alnylam's payer negotiation position.

Japan Expanded Access Data Published

A 2025 publication in Scientific Reports documents GIVLAARI efficacy and safety in Japanese AHP patients from an expanded access study. This signals:

  • Alnylam's interest in Asia-Pacific expansion
  • Potential for a Japan-focused ATU or market access study
  • Cross-cultural disease perception data that could complement the US/Germany/Brazil framework

No Competitive Pipeline Movement

  • AHP pipeline remains static. No new mechanisms approaching clinical development
  • GIVLAARI's first-in-class monopoly continues indefinitely
  • FDA designation of PANHEMATIN as an "essential medicine" secures hemin supply but does not change competitive dynamics

Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q1 2026

The AHP competitive landscape remains effectively uncontested. Alnylam's FY2025 earnings (Feb 12, 2026) confirmed the combined rare franchise at $500M with 15% patient growth — and no competitor is challenging GIVLAARI's position.

Recordati — Panhematin (hemin): Recordati's FY2025 results did not meaningfully feature Panhematin. Estimated annual revenue remains ~$30-40M, stable and non-threatening. The complementary use pattern persists: Panhematin for acute attack management, GIVLAARI for prophylactic prevention. With the medical community increasingly embracing AHP as a chronic disease (validated by the ATU Wave 4 chronicity shift data), the relationship between these therapies is additive rather than competitive — many patients on GIVLAARI still use Panhematin for breakthrough attacks.

Competitive reality for 2026: GIVLAARI faces zero competitive earnings pressure. The mid-single-digit 2026 growth guidance for the rare disease franchise reflects the natural pace of new patient identification in an ultra-rare disease, not competitive erosion. Alnylam's 2030 strategy positions rare disease as a "stable base" rather than a growth driver, which means GIVLAARI's commercial team will operate with lean resources — making ZoomRx's research all the more critical for tracking the only competition that matters: the undiagnosed patient pool.

3. ZoomRx Engagement: ATU Wave 5 Planning

Expected Scope Discussions

ATU Wave 5 kick-off likely in Q1-Q2 2026 for Q3 2026 fielding. ZoomRx should advocate for these design enhancements:

High priority (recommend strongly):

  • Maintain the 4-segment HCP profiling (Enthusiasts/Trialists/Converters/Inexperienced) for wave-over-wave tracking
  • Deeper Patient Type 4/5 module: actual prescribing behavior in chronic symptom and biochemically active patients, not just hypothetical preference
  • Expanded referral journey questions: how patients reach porphyria specialists, time from symptom onset to diagnosis (HCP-estimated)

Medium priority (propose if budget allows):

  • Qualitative follow-ups in Brazil specifically on structural access barriers (public vs. private payer, geographic access, in-home administration feasibility)
  • Larger Brazil sample (n=25+) to enable directional segment-level analysis
  • Cross-reference questions linking promotional exposure to treatment behavior (partial ATU+PET integration)

Proactive pitch opportunity:

  • A standalone diagnostic journey study targeting non-specialist physicians (PCPs, ER, general GI) who are the referral bottleneck. This would be a new study type for the GIVLAARI engagement and could justify incremental budget
  • A PET refresh (2+ years since last wave) with messaging updated for the chronicity narrative

Alnylam Contacts

  • Market Insights: Elizabeth Bergantino
  • ZoomRx team: Sneha Shah (Sr. Manager), Richard Durante (Principal), Pranav UT (Sr. Consultant)

4. Key Metrics to Track Going Forward

Metric Last Known Value Source Watch For
GIVLAARI patient growth rate ~15% YoY Alnylam earnings Deceleration below 10% = diagnostic ceiling
US GIVLAARI patient share Growing (+8% expected) ATU Wave 4 Actual vs. expected share growth in Wave 5
Germany GIVLAARI patient share Highest globally, growing (+9%) ATU Wave 4 Sustainability of growth; ceiling
Brazil patients on consistent Tx ~55% (45% untreated) ATU Wave 4 Any movement = structural access change
US Inexperienced segment size 47% of HCPs ATU Wave 4 Shrinkage = successful education/adoption
US disease perception (chronic vs. acute) Majority chronic ATU Wave 4 Stabilization or further shift
Off-label dosing rate 14% of US HCPs ATU Wave 4 Increase = signal for dosing study
Combined rare disease revenue $500M FY2025 Alnylam earnings Mid-single-digit growth delivered?

5. What Vinoth Should Know

Account Relationship

  • GIVLAARI is a recurring, stable engagement within the Alnylam account. Not high-drama, not at risk, but not growing either
  • The Alnylam 2030 strategy explicitly deprioritizes rare disease -- ZoomRx should position GIVLAARI research as lean and essential rather than expansive
  • The best growth play for the ZoomRx engagement is scope expansion: diagnostic journey study, PET refresh, integrated tracker, or Brazil access study. Each would be incremental revenue without requiring new headcount

Team Considerations

  • ZoomRx team (Sneha Shah, Pranav UT) has strong continuity on this engagement
  • Richard Durante provides senior oversight and strategic guidance
  • Sugandha Anand and Gauri Srivastava on project execution
  • This is a good training engagement for junior team members given the clear methodology (ATU/PET) and recurring cadence

Risk Watch

  • If Alnylam consolidates research budget toward TTR in line with the 2030 strategy, GIVLAARI ATU could move from annual to biennial cadence -- reducing revenue
  • Mitigation: proactively pitch an integrated or expanded scope before budget discussions happen. The ATU Wave 4 data provides strong rationale for why continued tracking and scope expansion are warranted

Cross-Account Learning

  • The "patient-finding" challenge in AHP is analogous to rare disease dynamics across other ZoomRx clients. Insights from the GIVLAARI engagement (HCP segmentation, diagnostic journey, access barriers) could inform methodology for other rare disease brands
  • The cross-market study design (US/Germany/Brazil) is a strong showcase for ZoomRx's global capabilities
